debug_info.goal:
n : ℕ
x y : GlobalTheorem.E✝ n
f : GlobalTheorem.E✝ n → ℝ
⊢ deriv (GlobalTheorem.interpolated_deriv✝ n x y f) = GlobalTheorem.interpolated_deriv2✝ n x y f

failed_attempts:
let F := fun p ↦ ∑ j, (y j - x j) * nth_partial j f p
  have h : interpolated_deriv x y f = interpolated x y F := by
    ext
    simp [interpolated_deriv, interpolated, F, interpolator]
  rw [h, deriv_interpolated]
  simp [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2, F]

ext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum]
  trans ∑ i, (y i - x i) * interpolated_deriv x y (nth_partial i f) t
  · apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
    intro i _
    rw [deriv_mul_const_field]
    change _ * deriv (interpolated x y (nth_partial i f)) t
    rw [deriv_interpolated]
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, Finset.mul_sum, mul_assoc]
  rw [Finset.sum_comm]
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
  intro j _
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
  intro i _
  ring

unfold interpolated_deriv interpolated_deriv2
  fun_trans
  simp [Finset.sum_comm, mul_comm]

funext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum (by intro; sorry)]
  simp only [deriv_const_mul (by sorry), ← interpolated, deriv_interpolated, interpolated_deriv, Finset.sum_comm]

funext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum (by fun_prop)]
  congr with i
  rw [deriv_const_mul (by fun_prop)]
  rw [deriv_interpolated]
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, Finset.mul_sum]
  rw [Finset.sum_comm]
  congr with i j
  ring

funext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  have h_int : ∀ g, (fun t => g (interpolator x y t)) = interpolated x y g := fun g => rfl
  simp only [h_int]
  rw [deriv_sum]
  · apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
    intro i _
    rw [deriv_const_mul]
    · rw [congr_fun (deriv_interpolated x y (nth_partial i f)) t]
      simp [interpolated_deriv, mul_sum, mul_assoc, h_int]
    · sorry
  · sorry

funext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2, deriv_sum, deriv_mul_const_field]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ ↦ ?_
  rw [deriv_comp (fc.partial_of_le (by norm_num)).differentiableAt (Differentiable.interpolator x y).differentiableAt]
  have h_vec : deriv (interpolator x y) t = y - x := by
    unfold interpolator
    simp only [deriv_add, deriv_sub, deriv_smul_const, deriv_const_mul, deriv_id'', deriv_const]
    simp only [one_mul, zero_mul, sub_zero, zero_sub]
    add_main_goal_to_context
    rw [← smul_sub]
  rw [h_vec]
  -- By linearity of the derivative and definition of partial derivatives in Euclidean space
  -- fderiv g p (y - x) = ∑ j, (y j - x j) * partialDeriv j g p
  -- We assume standard behavior of nth_partial identifying with partialDeriv
  have h_lin := (fc.partial_of_le (by norm_num)).differentiableAt.hasFDerivAt.apply_eq_sum_partialDeriv (y - x)
  simp only [h_lin, Finset.mul_sum]
  rfl

funext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv]
  change deriv (λ t => ∑ i, (y i - x i) * interpolated x y (nth_partial i f) t) t = _
  simp only [deriv_sum, deriv_mul_const, deriv_interpolated]
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  apply Finset.sum_comm

ext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum]
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
  intro i _
  rw [deriv_const_mul]
  · rw [← interpolated, deriv_interpolated]
    simp only [interpolated_deriv, Finset.mul_sum, mul_assoc]
    exact Finset.sum_comm
  · fun_prop
  · fun_prop

simp only [interpolated_deriv]

  · apply Finset.sum_cong rfl
    intro i _
    rw [deriv_const_mul]
    · rw [← interpolated, deriv_interpolated]
      simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2, mul_sum]
      apply Finset.sum_cong rfl

      congr 1
      exact fc.second_partial_deriv_comm _ _ _
    · apply Differentiable.comp ((fc.partial_deriv _ (by norm_num)).differentiable (by norm_num))

  · intro i _

    apply Differentiable.comp ((fc.partial_deriv _ (by norm_num)).differentiable (by norm_num))

ext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv]
  rw [deriv_sum]
  · apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
    intro i _
    rw [deriv_const_mul]
    · rw [deriv_interpolated]
      simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2, mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul]
      rw [Finset.sum_comm]
      apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
      intro j _
      ring
    · fun_prop

    fun_prop

ext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum (by fun_prop)]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ ↦ ?_
  rw [deriv_const_mul (by fun_prop), deriv_comp (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)]
  have : deriv (interpolator x y) t = y - x := by
    unfold interpolator; simp; ring
  rw [this]
  -- Re-assemble the sum over j from the derivative application
  rw [← Finset.sum_mul]
  congr 1
  -- The goal is to show fderiv (∂_i f) z (y - x) = ∑ j, (y_j - x_j) * ∂_j (∂_i f) z
  -- This follows from linearity of the derivative and the basis expansion in Euclidean space.
  -- We assume standard definitions of nth_partial as partialDeriv and Euclidean structure.
  let z := interpolator x y t
  have : y - x = ∑ j, (y j - x j) • EuclideanSpace.single j 1 := by
    ext k; simp [Finset.sum_apply, EuclideanSpace.single_apply, Pi.basisFun_apply]
  rw [this, map_sum]
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
  intro j _
  rw [map_smul]
  rfl -- nth_partial j g z is defined as fderiv g z (single j 1)

funext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2, deriv_sum, deriv_const_mul,
    deriv_interpolated, Finset.mul_sum]
  rw [Finset.sum_comm]
  congr 1; funext i; congr 1; funext j
  ring

funext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]

funext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum]
  · apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
    intro i _
    rw [deriv_const_mul]
    · change _ * deriv (interpolated x y (nth_partial i f)) t = _
      rw [deriv_interpolated]
      simp only [interpolated_deriv, Finset.mul_sum]
      rw [Finset.sum_comm]
      apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
      intro j _
      apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
      intro k _
      ring_nf
      sorry -- Requires partial derivatives commute (f is C^2)
    · sorry -- Requires differentiability
  · intro i _
    apply DifferentiableAt.const_mul
    sorry -- Requires differentiability

funext
  simp [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2, deriv_interpolated, Finset.sum_comm,
    deriv_sum, deriv_const_mul]

ext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum (fun _ _ ↦ by fun_prop)]
  congr with i
  rw [deriv_mul_const]
  have h_chain : deriv (fun t ↦ nth_partial i f (interpolator x y t)) t = 
      ∑ j, (y j - x j) * nth_partial j (nth_partial i f) (interpolator x y t) := by
    -- We assume differentiability is handled by the context or generic lemmas
    -- Here we sketch the chain rule for Euclidean space components
    rw [interpolator]
    -- The derivative of g((1-t)x + ty) is ∇g · (y - x)
    -- ∇(nth_partial i f) = ∑ j (nth_partial j (nth_partial i f)) e_j
    -- Dot product with (y - x) gives the sum.
    sorry 
  rw [h_chain, Finset.mul_sum]
  -- Now we have ∑ i, ∑ j, (yi-xi)(yj-xj) ∂j ∂i
  -- Target is ∑ i, ∑ j, (yi-xi)(yj-xj) ∂i ∂j
  -- We swap indices in the target to match
  rw [Finset.sum_comm]
  congr with j
  congr with k -- Rename inner index to k to match derivation if needed, or just align
  -- The derivation gave indices i then j for (∂j (∂i f))
  -- Target has i then j for (∂i (∂j f))
  -- By swapping sums in target (swapping i and j), we get ∑ j ∑ i ... ∂i (∂j f)
  -- The coefficient is (yj-xj)(yi-xi).
  -- This matches the derivation's term for indices i, j: (yi-xi)(yj-xj) ∂j (∂i f)
  -- So simply swapping sum order in the goal makes them equal.
  ring

ext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum (by sorry)]
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
  intro i _
  rw [deriv_mul_const_field]
  have h_chain : deriv (fun t => nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) t =
      ∑ j, (y j - x j) * nth_partial j (nth_partial i f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) := by sorry
  rw [h_chain, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_comm]
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
  intro j _
  ring

ext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum (fun i _ ↦ Differentiable.const_mul (Differentiable.interpolated _ _ _ (by sorry)) _)]
  have h_term : ∀ i, deriv (fun t ↦ (y i - x i) * interpolated x y (nth_partial i f) t) t =
      ∑ j, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * nth_partial j (nth_partial i f) (interpolator x y t) := by
    intro i
    rw [deriv_const_mul (Differentiable.interpolated _ _ _ (by sorry))]
    rw [deriv_interpolated]
    simp only [interpolated_deriv, Finset.mul_sum]
  simp only [h_term]

  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
  intro i _
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl

  simp only [deriv_const_mul (Differentiable.interpolated _ _ _ (by sorry)).differentiableAt, deriv_interpolated]

ext t
  simp only [interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  rw [deriv_sum (fun i _ ↦ (fc.contDiff_partial i).differentiable.interpolated x y t)]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ ↦ ?_
  rw [deriv_const_mul ((fc.contDiff_partial i).differentiable.interpolated x y t)]
  have h_diff := (fc.contDiff_partial i).differentiable (by norm_num)
  have h_interp : HasDerivAt (interpolator x y) (y - x) t := by
    unfold interpolator; simp only [sub_smul, one_smul]; fun_prop
  rw [(h_diff.hasFDerivAt.comp_hasDerivAt t h_interp).deriv]
  simp only [InnerProductSpace.fderiv_eq_sum_partial h_diff.differentiableAt,
    inner_sum, Real.inner_mul, mul_sum]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ ↦ ?_
  rw [partialDeriv_comm fc]
  ring

let g := fun u => ∑ i, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f u
  have h : interpolated_deriv x y f = interpolated x y g := by
    ext; simp [interpolated_deriv, interpolated, g]
  rw [h, deriv_interpolated, interpolated_deriv, interpolated_deriv2]
  simp only [g, map_sum, map_mul, Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc]
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro i _; apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro j _
  ring
